domain: Roman aqueduct constant-gradient siphoning (Aqua Claudia 1:258 mean gradient over 50+ km; inverted siphon for valley crossings via communicating-vessels pressure; uniform low-slope maintained over thousands of segments)
specific_mechanism: Strict constant gradient over very long distance maintains phase-coherent water flow; inverted-siphon bypass for unavoidable obstacles preserves system-level coherence.
llm_application: AQUEDUCT-GRADIENT: a long-context pretraining regulariser. For input length T tokens, compute the L2 gradient norm ‖∂L/∂e_t‖ at each token-position embedding e_t. The added regulariser is λ × Var_t(‖∂L/∂e_t‖) — the VARIANCE of per-position gradient norms — pressuring the model to keep gradient flow uniform across the entire context (preventing the 'lost-in-the-middle' attenuation). Combined with INVERTED-SIPHON: every K layers, a learnable low-rank residual bypass connects input embedding e_t directly to layer-K output at position t (R_t = LoRA_low_rank(e_t)), strengthening direct long-range path. Differs from Lighthouse Attention multi-res pooling, MLA latent compression, and Latent-Condensed by being a GRADIENT-NORM UNIFORMITY OBJECTIVE applied at training time across positions.
novelty_claim: Lighthouse Attention uses hierarchical multi-resolution selection. MLA compresses K/V into latent space. Latent-Condensed reduces sequence length via condensation. Lost-in-the-middle papers diagnose attenuation; few prescribe fixes. AQUEDUCT-GRADIENT differs by (a) explicit POSITION-WISE gradient-norm VARIANCE PENALTY in pretraining loss, (b) INVERTED-SIPHON low-rank residual bypass at strategic depths, (c) phase-coherent long-distance flow goal. Combination not seen.
